steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states: ‘on average, studies of fish showed larger differences than studies of birds did, [but] for every class of animals examined, there were individual studies showing differences well above the average for fish.’ The blank must follow logically from both parts — fish lead on average, but some bird studies surpass even the fish average.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "the studies in the meta-analysis that examined fish were more likely than those that examined birds to specify whether the observed effects were detrimental.": The passage never discusses whether studies reported effects as detrimental. This introduces information not in the text. ✗
  • "the differences that studies attribute to exposure to anthropogenic noise are likely to be more pronounced for birds than they are for fish.": This reverses the passage. Fish showed larger differences on average — the opposite of what the differences that studies attribute to exposure to anthropogenic noise are likely to be more pronounced for birds than they are for fish. claims. ✗
  • "the difference found in the study conducted by Lidia Eva Wysocki and colleagues was likely larger than the average difference for studies of common carp included in the meta analysis.": Wysocki studied common carp (fish), but the passage gives no information about how Wysocki’s specific result compared to the meta-analysis fish average. This is unsupported. ✗
  • "some studies of birds found larger effects of exposure to anthropogenic noise than some studies of fish did.": If some bird studies exceeded the fish average, those bird studies necessarily surpassed all fish studies that were at or below the average — so some bird studies did find larger effects than some fish studies. This follows directly and necessarily from the passage. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
